name: Almería – circular route through the city
name_original: Almería – ruta circular por la ciudad
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by dramatic volcanic coastal formations, including rocky cliffs and pristine coves. Inland, the region features an arid desertscape with rolling hills and valleys, alongside unique fossil dunes and ancient volcanic craters. The area also includes the Salinas de Cabo de Gata, a significant wetland for migratory birds. This varied terrain offers a range of easy routes for exploration. Best easy hiking trails around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax The most popular easy hiking route is Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Caldera de la Majada Redonda from Presillas Bajas (SL-A 101), a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This route explores the volcanic landscape of Cabo de Gata Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro del Cautivo – Tabernas Oasis loop from Puente del Cautivo, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail offers views of the arid desertscape and rolling hills. Local hikers also love the Almería Seafront Promenade – Parque de la Estación loop from Almeria, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through urban coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax is defined by volcanic coastal landscapes, arid desertscapes, and significant wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy paths. The routes in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2200 reviews. More than 9800 hikers have used komoot to explore Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: There are over 160 easy hiking trails in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax, offering a wide variety of landscapes from volcanic coasts to arid desertscapes. In total, the region boasts more than 360 hiking routes for all abilities.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: Easy hikes in this region showcase dramatic volcanic coastal landscapes with rocky cliffs and pristine coves, as well as arid desertscapes featuring rolling hills, valleys, and unique fossil dunes. You can also find routes near significant wetlands like the Salinas de Cabo de Gata.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many easy trails are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38262906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Cautivo – Tabernas Oasis loop from Puente del Cautivo&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the arid desertscape, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1283851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient olive tree of Agua Amarga – Agua Amarga Beach loop from Agua Amarga&lt;/a&gt; provides a coastal experience.

Q: What are some interesting historical sites or landmarks I can see on an easy hike?
A: You can explore historical sites like the old gold mines on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10307071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodalquilar Gold Mines – Rodalquilar Gold Mine Ruins loop from Rodalquilar&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region features several castles and historical towers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2846137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Almería&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felipe Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest near some trails.

Q: Are there easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the focus on easy trails makes many routes suitable for families. Coastal promenades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31821280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almería Seafront Promenade – Parque de la Estación loop from Almeria&lt;/a&gt; are generally flat and accessible. Shorter, well-maintained paths in the Cabo de Gata Natural Park are also great options for a family outing.

Q: Can I find easy hikes that offer coastal views or access to beaches?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning volcanic coastline. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14603065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Isleta del Moro – Arco Beach loop from Cerro de la Amatista&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40380133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Sirenas Viewpoint – Sirens' Reef loop from Cabo de Gata&lt;/a&gt; provide beautiful sea views and often lead to picturesque coves and beaches.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: The easy trails in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from dramatic coastlines to unique desert landscapes, and the accessibility of the paths.

Q: Are there any easy routes that pass by unique natural features like volcanic craters?
A: Yes, the region's volcanic past means you can find trails that explore these features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984529987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Caldera de la Majada Redonda from Presillas Bajas (SL-A 101)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, traversing the volcanic landscape of Cabo de Gata Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy trails where I might see wildlife, especially birds?
A: The Salinas de Cabo de Gata are a crucial stop for numerous migratory bird species, including flamingos. While no specific easy route is listed here, many coastal trails in the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, such as those near the Salinas, offer opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of an easy hike in this area?
A: Easy hikes in Campo Níjar y Bajo Andarax typically range from 4 to 8 kilometers (2.5 to 5 miles) and can be completed in about 1.5 to 2.5 hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torregarcía Hermitage – Garcia Tower loop from Retamar&lt;/a&gt; is about 7.6 km and takes around 2 hours.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many coastal trails provide stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the dramatic volcanic landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Playa de los Muertos – Mesa Roldán Lighthouse loop from Aparcamiento Playa de los Muertos&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive vistas, including the iconic Playa de los Muertos.
tips: Sculptures in the narrow park-like green strip between the two lanes of the main street Avenida FG Lorca
The building, from the end of the 19th century, is worth it in itself as a good sample of "iron architecture" but here you can of course find good fresh and local produce.

The Alcazaba of Almeria is a Moorish fortress that overlooks the entire city of Almeria.
The Alcazaba was built under Caliph Abd ar-Rahman III in the 10th century. built on a 85 m high hill in the center of the city. Since the city was formally founded in 955, it has housed the residence of the city lords. During the Taifa period from 1012 to 1091, when the city was independent, it became the residence of sovereign princes who temporarily claimed the title of caliph. Al-Mutasim in particular made a name for himself as a builder from 1051 to 1091.
The Alcazaba was used by Muslim governors up until the 15th century. In 1522 the palace complex was destroyed by an earthquake and partially replaced by new buildings. In the course of these reconstruction measures, parts of the Alcazaba were shaped by Christian architecture and the mosque was converted into a chapel.

The Cathedral of the Incarnation in Almería, also known as the Holy and Apostolic Cathedral-Fortress Church of the Incarnation, is an architectural gem that combines elements of late Gothic, Renaissance, Baroque, and Neoclassical architecture. It was built between 1524 and 1562, and its design includes defensive features such as battlements and turrets, reflecting the attacks of Barbary pirates at the time.

The Paseo Marítimo de Almería, also known as Paseo Carmen de Burgos, is an ideal place to enjoy the coast and the Mediterranean atmosphere. This promenade extends for approximately 2.5 kilometres, from the mouth of the Andarax River to the Port of Almería.

The nice "Parque de la Estación" is located directly behind the train station or as an extension of the same with the original tracks, which are now overgrown by plants and trees. Again in a further extension of the park, the original rail lines run elevated up into the port.

waypoints.name: Plaza de las Velas sculptures, Almería
